project_type: Python Flask web app with SQLAlchemy ORM, background job system
test_framework: pytest with pytest-socket (blocks network by default), SQLite in-memory for testing
build_command: pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
verification_instructions: Run python -m pytest tests/ -v and ensure all tests pass. No docker or CI steps needed locally.
setup_instructions: pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Python 3.13 available via pyenv. No Docker needed.
contribution_requirements: Line length 120 chars, ruff for linting, isort with Black profile, type hints used. Tests use pytest fixtures from tests/conftest.py. The app uses TestingConfig with SQLite in-memory for tests.
key_patterns:
- SQLAlchemy models in flask_app/main_app/db/models/ use Column() declarative style
- Services in flask_app/main_app/db/services/ handle DB operations
- IntegrityError is caught in admin_service.py with rollback pattern
- Custom exceptions in flask_app/main_app/db/exceptions.py
- db_guard decorator in services/utils.py catches exceptions and rolls back
- Tests use app fixture from conftest.py with app_context()
- Integration tests use _clean_db fixture to reset tables between tests
directory_structure:
flask_app/main_app/ - main application code
flask_app/main_app/db/models/ - SQLAlchemy models
flask_app/main_app/db/services/ - DB service layer (CRUD operations)
flask_app/main_app/db/exceptions.py - custom DB exceptions
flask_app/main_app/new_jobs/ - background job system
flask_app/main_app/app_routes/ - Flask blueprint routes
tests/unit/ - unit tests
tests/integration/ - integration tests
